briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon, everyone. I will now begin the climate briefing for April 13, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Climate Crisis and Global Ecosystem Warnings
sections.content: Today's climate-related news warns of the intensifying direct impacts of climate change in various parts of the world. The Antarctic ecosystem is severely disrupted, with the extinction risk levels of Emperor Penguins and Antarctic fur seals being elevated [5], while in Korea, mass die-offs of Korean fir trees on Jirisan's Banyabong Peak are raising concerns about the collapse of the alpine ecosystem [15]. The case of super typhoons in the Philippines serves as a warning of climate change to Korea [14], and heavy rainfall in southern China is also analyzed as part of this warning [27]. In Japan, a historic poor harvest of oyster production and an increase in bear sightings have also been suggested as potentially linked to climate change [32, 33], and analyses suggest that even the hosting of future Winter Olympics is threatened by climate change [146]. These reports clearly demonstrate that the climate crisis is no longer a distant future concern but a present reality directly threatening our lives and ecosystems.

sections.title: 🌱 Domestic Climate Change Response Efforts and Acceleration of Carbon Neutrality Implementation
sections.content: Domestically, diverse efforts are actively underway to respond to the climate crisis and achieve carbon neutrality. The Climate Change Center has submitted policy recommendations to the National Assembly to strengthen reduction implementation [1], and the National Assembly's Special Committee on Climate Crisis has received the final results from the Carbon Neutrality Act Amendment Deliberation Committee and plans to pursue amendments within April [77, 89]. During this process, citizens strongly demanded that greenhouse gas emissions be reduced 'quickly and significantly' [36, 45, 79, 81, 91, 96, 141]. Local governments nationwide are operating Climate Change Weeks to spread low-carbon lifestyle practices [2, 6, 7, 9, 12, 17, 20, 24, 63], and the Ministry of SMEs and Startups is encouraging the industrial sector's transition by investing 18.1 billion KRW to support carbon-neutral facility investments and recruiting supply chain cooperation companies [37, 38, 42, 50, 52, 53, 56, 58, 60, 66, 72, 85, 99, 100, 103, 106]. Furthermore, the leading role of public institutions is prominent, with Busan Urban Corporation achieving a 47.8% greenhouse gas reduction rate and Korea Southern Power raising its 2035 greenhouse gas reduction target to 72% [39, 65, 88].

sections.title: ⚠️ Challenges in Domestic Climate Policy Implementation and Demands for Improvement
sections.content: Despite these efforts, challenges and demands for improvement continue to arise in the implementation of domestic climate policies. Significant disparities in carbon neutrality efforts among cities and counties within Gyeonggi Province have led to criticism of a 'superficial system' [34, 78], and the occurrence of rust and corrosion at the Gongju Rest Area's solar power facility, which should be a symbol of carbon neutrality, has brought Korea Expressway Corporation's management shortcomings under scrutiny [67]. Furthermore, Climate Emergency Action urged substantial reduction efforts through a press conference on the results of the public deliberation for the amendment of the Framework Act on Carbon Neutrality [68]. This demonstrates that despite strong public demands for reduction, issues of policy effectiveness and management deficiencies persist in practice, suggesting the need for more robust and consistent policy implementation.

sections.title: 📰 Era of Energy Security, Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition
sections.content: Instability in international affairs, such as the Middle East conflict, is highlighting the importance of energy security and accelerating the transition to renewable energy [109, 112, 118, 123, 130, 134, 154]. China's climate envoy emphasized that renewable energy is effective in ensuring energy security [122], and Australia's Minister for Climate Change and Energy also stated that the fuel crisis 'underscores' the need for electrification [153]. Domestically, the Korea Power Exchange is holding a power regulation governance discussion forum to prepare for the '100GW Renewable Energy Era,' discussing future energy systems [108, 128, 132], and various local governments, including Yecheon County, are accelerating electricity bill savings and strengthening energy welfare through integrated renewable energy support projects [110, 111, 115, 116, 117, 119, 120, 121, 126, 127, 129, 131, 135, 136]. Specific movements to expand renewable energy are emerging in various sectors, such as converting abandoned mine purification facilities into renewable energy facilities [124], and NH Nonghyup Bank providing large-scale financial support for offshore wind power projects [138, 140].

sections.title: 🌍 International Climate Action and Current Challenges
sections.content: The international community is also continuing its awareness and response efforts regarding climate change, but simultaneously faces several challenges. In Richmond, USA, runners participated in a 5K race, engaging in activities to combat climate change [143], and McGill University demonstrated adaptation efforts by conducting research on climate-resilient agricultural cultivation through new greenhouses [151]. In the CamSur region of the Philippines, clean cooking stoves are being used, contributing to emission reductions [147]. However, news that the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC) is facing a funding crisis [149] and reports of significant cuts to climate change response budgets in the Trump administration's proposed budget [21] raise concerns about the sustainability of international climate action. Thus, while the international community responds to the climate crisis through citizen participation and scientific research, it also grapples with the realistic challenges of political and financial difficulties.

briefing.closing: This concludes today's climate briefing for April 13, 2026. We look forward to seeing you again at the next briefing.
